jokes.text: Congratulations. It's meat cat! the cheesy blasters mascot. I'm what's inside ya! Razzmatazz!
jokes.setup: Liz is having a dream about giving birth
jokes.punchline: Instead of a baby, she gives birth to a junk food mascot
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist opening that immediately establishes Liz's relationship with junk food through dream metaphor. The mascot's enthusiastic delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I got to eat better.
jokes.setup: After the absurd junk food birth dream
jokes.punchline: Liz's understated reaction to her obvious problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan understatement that shows Liz's self-awareness but lack of follow-through. Great economy.

jokes.text: While I tell you a funny story About my friend's cat. His name is Mr. Wiggles, And his cat's name is Benson.
jokes.setup: Kenneth offers to tell a story about his friend's cat
jokes.punchline: The misdirection reveal that Mr. Wiggles is the friend, not the cat
jokes.explanation: Classic Kenneth absurdist logic with perfect misdirection structure. Could only come from this character.

jokes.text: She was trying to use An Oxycontin prescription bottle as I.D.
jokes.setup: Security called about Jenna's mother trying to get in
jokes.punchline: Using a drug prescription as identification
jokes.explanation: Dark but perfectly establishes Verna's character in one detail. Economical character writing.

jokes.text: Just like the day I was born.
jokes.setup: Jenna describes her mother sitting on a curb chain-smoking
jokes.punchline: Adding 'just like the day I was born' makes it about her birth
jokes.explanation: Exceptional dark comedy callback that recontextualizes the previous image. Perfect economy and character integration.

jokes.text: This is some woman who shows up Every couple of years to ask for money. To her, I'm just a gorgeous, Naturally-blonde ATM.
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining her relationship with her mother
jokes.punchline: Describing herself as a 'gorgeous, naturally-blonde ATM' with typical Jenna vanity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna moment - even when describing exploitation, she can't help but compliment herself.

jokes.text: don't go to sleep with a frown in your pocket
jokes.setup: Liz trying to share wisdom about mother-daughter relationships
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical folk wisdom that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd fake folk wisdom that sounds profound but is meaningless. Very quotable.

jokes.text: I left my Sith Lord cloak Too near my mom's shrine to Italian Jesus, And the candles started a fire.
jokes.setup: Frank explaining why there were firemen at his house
jokes.punchline: The collision of Star Wars nerddom and Italian Catholic kitsch causing a fire
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank character detail - the specific clash of his interests with his mother's creates perfect absurdity.

jokes.text: I'm sorry. Would you like to stay in my guest house? Yeah. Thanks. When's good to go over there? Oh, I'm not offering. I'm just taking a survey To gauge general interest.
jokes.setup: Tracy appears to offer Frank a place to stay
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was just conducting a fake survey, not actually offering
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection joke with perfect Tracy self-centeredness. Strong structure.

jokes.text: Oh, I'd like to pre-apologize For clogging your tub, sink, and toilet.
jokes.setup: Frank thanking Liz for letting him stay
jokes.punchline: Immediate gross overshare about his bathroom habits
jokes.explanation: Solid gross-out humor that fits Frank's character. Economical but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Mothers. You can't kill them...
jokes.setup: Jack making a statement about mothers
jokes.punchline: The implication that he's considered it, delivered deadpan
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack darkness delivered with characteristic understatement. Great economy.

jokes.text: Keep you and your pets safe With gator hater gator spray. Sorry. Honey, you got to get closer to the gator. We drove eleven hours to get here, And you're not getting any younger.
jokes.setup: Flashback to young Jenna doing a commercial
jokes.punchline: Verna pushing her daughter closer to actual danger for money
jokes.explanation: Dark mother-daughter dynamic perfectly established through this commercial flashback.

jokes.text: that day in January When the post office is closed.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining what his mother calls Martin Luther King Day
jokes.punchline: Colleen's obliviously racist reduction of the holiday
jokes.explanation: Perfect characterization of Colleen's casual racism through bureaucratic language.

jokes.text: Can I just tell you I'm genuinely having fun right now?
jokes.setup: Jack teaching Jenna how to deal with her manipulative mother
jokes.punchline: Jenna treating serious emotional advice like entertainment
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna character moment - her narcissism makes her miss the emotional weight.

jokes.text: Say no. Talk low. Let her go.
jokes.setup: Jack's three-step process for dealing with manipulative mothers
jokes.punchline: The rhyming advice that sounds like a self-help mantra
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and structure. The rhyming makes it memorably ridiculous while actually being decent advice. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Wow. An old white lady yelling at me While wearing microwavable socks. This is so different than living with my mom.
jokes.setup: Liz lecturing Frank about smoking
jokes.punchline: Frank sarcastically noting the similarity to his mother while mentioning her weird socks
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor with the microwavable socks detail adding specificity.

jokes.text: That the episode of diff'rent strokes About Dudley dad Has proven incontrovertibly That smoking destroys your health And leads to ridicule from Willis.
jokes.setup: Liz explaining why smoking is bad
jokes.punchline: Using a very specific 80s sitcom episode as scientific proof
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist reference that shows Liz's TV-addled brain. Very specific and quotable.

jokes.text: manufactured in a facility that also processes food.
jokes.setup: Frank reading a junk food label
jokes.punchline: The implication that the product isn't actually food
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor about processed food labeling. Great economy.

jokes.text: This is what meat cat spoke of.
jokes.setup: Liz realizing she's in junk food trouble
jokes.punchline: Treating her dream mascot as prophetic
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that treats the absurd dream as prophecy. Perfect economy.

jokes.text: But if doing this restores my sex drive, The plus-sized ladies in the mail room Have you to blame.
jokes.setup: Frank agreeing to quit smoking and eat better
jokes.punchline: Immediate overshare about his sexual plans
jokes.explanation: Typical Frank gross-out humor. Fits character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Is this the fight? I'm talking quieter. I'm in control.
jokes.setup: Jenna trying to use Jack's advice during confrontation with her mother
jokes.punchline: Jenna narrating her own strategy out loud, defeating the purpose
jokes.explanation: Great meta-comedy as Jenna's narcissism makes her announce her strategy, ruining it.

jokes.text: Don't go, mommy. I'll stand closer to the gator.
jokes.setup: Jenna's mother being unexpectedly kind and saying she's proud
jokes.punchline: Jenna reverting to childhood with reference to the gator commercial
jokes.explanation: Brilliant emotional callback that shows Jenna's vulnerability through the gator reference. Exceptional character work.

jokes.text: funny story. There was a guy on the subway Who I thought for a second didn't have any fingers. But then I realized He was holding his hand like this.
jokes.setup: Kenneth apologizing for being late with a 'funny story'
jokes.punchline: The anticlimatic revelation about the finger misunderstanding
jokes.explanation: Classic Kenneth non-story that's funny because of how mundane it is. Pure character comedy.

jokes.text: While you were talking, I put a thumbtack in my neck. Makes me feel something.
jokes.setup: Pete explaining he needs a stress release since Kenneth took his away
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's self-harming with office supplies for emotional release
jokes.explanation: Dark and unexpected character development for Pete. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Autumn tempeh risotto With salmon-rubbed streusel chunks.
jokes.setup: Liz describing her healthy meal delivery
jokes.punchline: The pretentious and unappetizing description of 'healthy' food
jokes.explanation: Good satire of pretentious health food marketing. Specific enough to be funny.

jokes.text: It turns out she had a tumor Pressing on her brain's pleasure center. A tumor those quack doctors removed.
jokes.setup: Explaining why his mother seemed to have changed in the 90s
jokes.punchline: A brain tumor made her nicer, and Jack resents its removal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy that perfectly captures Jack's relationship with his mother. Calling doctors 'quacks' for removing a helpful tumor is hilariously twisted.

jokes.text: He does look like he'd be great Between those two things we were talking about.
jokes.setup: Verna meeting Jack for the first time
jokes.punchline: Immediate inappropriate sexual comment about Jack
jokes.explanation: Establishes Verna's crudeness immediately. Functional character work but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Something's killing all the birds in my neighborhood.
jokes.setup: Jack complimenting Verna's earrings
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation that she makes jewelry from dead birds
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark character detail delivered with perfect casualness. Great economy and originality.

jokes.text: Could a bad mom have raised a daughter Who was engaged to a congressman when she was 16?
jokes.setup: Verna defending herself as a good mother
jokes.punchline: Using child engagement to a politician as proof of good parenting
jokes.explanation: Dark and twisted logic that perfectly captures Verna's warped values. Excellent character writing.

jokes.text: Like a pair of white jeans And a Dan Marino jersey or something, 'kay?
jokes.setup: Telling Jack to wear something nice to the party
jokes.punchline: Her idea of fancy clothing being white jeans and a football jersey
jokes.explanation: Perfect class commentary. The specificity of Dan Marino makes it funnier and more character-specific than just 'sports jersey.'

jokes.text: Last night I spent an hour Trying to remember how to spell the word 'height.'
jokes.setup: Liz describing withdrawal symptoms from junk food
jokes.punchline: The specific cognitive failure of forgetting how to spell a simple word
jokes.explanation: Relatable and specific detail about mental fog. Good observational humor.

jokes.text: Rossitanos have refused to fight in seven wars.
jokes.setup: Frank claiming to have strong willpower
jokes.punchline: Using his family's history of cowardice as evidence of strength
jokes.explanation: Brilliant twisted logic that reframes cowardice as willpower. Perfect Frank character work and highly quotable.

jokes.text: Just like the one our nanny used To catch me watching her sleep.
jokes.setup: Tracy comparing a surveillance idea to a nanny cam
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of creepy behavior toward his nanny
jokes.explanation: Typical Tracy inappropriate overshare. Good character consistency but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I was told I'd get paid If I came here and danced while the Atlanta hawks eat dinner.
jokes.setup: A professional dancer arriving at what appears to be Jenna's party
jokes.punchline: Revealing she was hired for a completely different, absurd job
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist misdirection. The specificity of the Hawks makes it funnier.

jokes.text: A liar. A dental hygienist.
jokes.setup: Verna listing things she's been in life
jokes.punchline: Treating 'dental hygienist' as equally shameful as 'liar'
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Verna's skewed values through parallel structure.

jokes.text: I've been a lot of things- A liar. A dental hygienist.
jokes.setup: Verna listing her life roles
jokes.punchline: Equating being a liar with having a legitimate profession
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy that treats moral failings and career equally. The specificity of dental hygienist makes it funnier than a generic job.

jokes.text: And then I went and drove my jet ski Into that great big bunch of moss.
jokes.setup: Verna explaining why she missed the pageant
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous image of crashing a jet ski into moss
jokes.explanation: Absurd and specific detail that perfectly captures Verna's chaotic lifestyle.

jokes.text: Because I'm listening to the words.
jokes.setup: Someone asking Jack why he's not moved by the mother-daughter duet
jokes.punchline: Jack's analytical approach ruins the emotional moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack character moment - his cynicism expressed through logical analysis.

jokes.text: I feel like I'm in The Martha Stewart section of Kmart.
jokes.setup: Verna commenting on Jack's office
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment comparing luxury to discount store
jokes.explanation: Good class-based humor that shows Verna's limited frame of reference.

jokes.text: I did not drive my home All the way to new york city Just to kiss my daughter's ass.
jokes.setup: Verna seeming to be genuine about her relationship with Jenna
jokes.punchline: Revealing her crude true motivation and living situation
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that reveals Verna's true character while also suggesting she lives in her car.

jokes.text: Topless arguments. Infections caused by jacuzzi water.
jokes.setup: Verna pitching what their reality show would include
jokes.punchline: Immediately going to inappropriate and gross content
jokes.explanation: Standard gross-out humor that fits Verna but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: It's a mermaid. Doing it with Captain Morgan.
jokes.setup: Jack guessing what tattoo Verna has on her sleeve
jokes.punchline: Verna's confirmation of the crude imagery
jokes.explanation: Predictable crude humor. Fits character but lacks sophistication.

jokes.text: Which is in my underpanties.
jokes.setup: Saying there's more money where that came from
jokes.punchline: Revealing she keeps money in her underwear
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out humor that fits Verna's character. 'Underpanties' is particularly childish and unsexy language for the situation.

jokes.text: My mom just made them. So they still might have some pigeon mites. But she said those can't affect-affect humans-humans.
jokes.setup: Jack commenting on Jenna's new earrings
jokes.punchline: The gross detail about mites and Jenna's uncertain repetition
jokes.explanation: Callback to the dead bird earrings with added gross detail. Jenna's uncertainty adds to the humor.

jokes.text: She's made a full 360.
jokes.setup: Jack saying Verna made a 180-degree change
jokes.punchline: Jenna incorrectly saying 360, which would mean no change at all
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jenna malapropism that accidentally reveals the truth about her mother. Perfect economy and character work.

jokes.text: Frank's old cigarettes. When I went to bed last night, There were 15 cigarettes in the pack. This morning, there were 12.
jokes.setup: Liz setting up evidence that Frank has been cheating
jokes.punchline: The precise counting suggests obsessive behavior
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the revelation to come. Shows Liz's obsessiveness.

jokes.text: They're called night spanx.
jokes.setup: Frank asking what Liz is wearing while sleepwalking
jokes.punchline: Both are wearing shapewear to bed
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag about vanity extending to sleepwear. The fact that they both wear them makes it funnier.

jokes.text: It's a little-understood parasomnial disorder.
jokes.setup: Explaining Liz's sleepwalking
jokes.punchline: Frank using medical terminology incorrectly for sleep eating
jokes.explanation: Decent use of pseudo-medical language. Frank trying to sound smart while describing gross behavior fits his character.

jokes.text: They're called night spanx.
jokes.setup: Someone asking what Liz is wearing in the video
jokes.punchline: The embarrassing revelation about her sleepwear
jokes.explanation: Good embarrassing character detail. The simultaneous response suggests both are wearing them.

jokes.text: You're sleepwalking, Liz. It's a little-understood parasomnial disorder.
jokes.setup: Trying to explain Liz's behavior in the video
jokes.punchline: Frank using medical terminology incorrectly
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection as Frank tries to cover for Liz's sleep eating.

jokes.text: Did you learn nothing from Jurassic Park?
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining that Liz's body found a way around her restrictions
jokes.punchline: Using Jurassic Park philosophy for eating disorders
jokes.explanation: Brilliant application of Jurassic Park's 'life finds a way' to sleep eating. Very quotable.

jokes.text: I don't mean to be the black guy at the movie, But you better move, girl!
jokes.setup: Tracy warning Liz about something happening in the video
jokes.punchline: Self-aware reference to movie audience stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on racial stereotypes in horror movies while still fulfilling the trope.

jokes.text: I'm eating it.
jokes.setup: Everyone thinking Liz smoked the cigarettes
jokes.punchline: The even more disgusting revelation that she ate them
jokes.explanation: Exceptional escalation that's both gross and perfectly in character. Great economy of language for maximum impact.

jokes.text: Denise, I said wait upstairs. My boss is asleep in the other room. I was told You have a package for the mail room.
jokes.setup: Frank appearing to have company while Liz sleeps
jokes.punchline: The euphemistic 'package for the mail room' conversation
jokes.explanation: Standard sexual innuendo but fits the character and situation well.

jokes.text: I need you, Denise. I'm trying to quit smoking, And my mom's not here. I'm your mama now, baby.
jokes.setup: Frank's emotional vulnerability being revealed
jokes.punchline: The inappropriate mother-son roleplay
jokes.explanation: Disturbing character revelation that explains Frank's 'release' needs. Effective cringe comedy.

jokes.text: I want to be in business with you.
jokes.setup: After seeing Verna's manipulative behavior
jokes.punchline: Jack deciding to partner with her instead of protecting Jenna
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack move - seeing opportunity where others see problems. Good misdirection.

jokes.text: Just like when I dye my pool on st. Patrick's day.
jokes.setup: Verna saying they'll be 'swimming in green'
jokes.punchline: The literal and absurd detail about dyeing her pool
jokes.explanation: Good character detail that shows Verna's tackiness in a specific way.

jokes.text: And I believe that gives me the upper hand,
jokes.setup: Verna turning Jack's own advice against him
jokes.punchline: Using Jack's 'upper hand' phrase from his Jenna training
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that shows Verna turning Jack's own manipulation tactics against him.

jokes.text: 3 grand a visit, And a rain tarp for my washer and dryer.
jokes.setup: Verna negotiating her payment for being a good mother
jokes.punchline: The specific and pathetic additional demand
jokes.explanation: Perfect character detail that shows Verna's priorities and living situation.

jokes.text: If you are spending your mornings with Kenneth, What's your release?
jokes.setup: Tracy asking Pete about his stress release now that Kenneth interrupts his coffee time
jokes.punchline: Setting up Pete's disturbing nail-biting revelation
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Pete's reveal. Continues the episode's theme about everyone needing releases.

jokes.text: I've been biting my nails. Oh. I feel alive!
jokes.setup: Pete revealing his new stress release
jokes.punchline: The intensity of his reaction to nail biting
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Pete's progression from thumbtacks to nail biting. His enthusiasm makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You're a good man, Jack Donaghy. Thanks, meat cat.
jokes.setup: Jack being complimented on his Verna solution
jokes.punchline: Meat cat appearing to thank Jack, bringing the episode full circle
jokes.explanation: Brilliant bookend callback that brings the absurd dream mascot back to comment on the resolution. Perfect structure.
